description: Canonical, tool-agnostic agent execution hook policy for this repository. This file is documentation of intent; each coding agent's native hook mechanism (e.g. Claude Code's .claude/settings.json) is the actually-enforced mirror of the policy described here.

action.input: JSON tool-call payload on stdin (tool_name, tool_input.command)
action.onMatch: block the tool call and surface the reason to the agent
action.onNoMatch: allow the tool call to proceed

hooks.purpose: Block obviously destructive shell commands (e.g. rm -rf /, mkfs, raw disk writes, fork bombs) before they execute, as a mid-trajectory gate distinct from commit-time hooks in .pre-commit-config.yaml.
hooks.scopeNote: Best-effort denylist, not a sandbox — it does not parse shell semantics and can be bypassed by obfuscated commands. See the script's module docstring for the full caveat.
